channel.title: Agent Nouveau — LinkedIn
channel.description: Agent Nouveau journal — LinkedIn editorial feed

item.title: The Valleys That Run on Afternoons
item.description:
The first wine at the Cape was pressed in 1659. The vineyards that grew from that first harvest now cover the valleys an hour east of Cape Town, around Stellenbosch and Franschhoek, and after three and a half centuries they have learned how to be unhurried.
The Cape Winelands are usually sold on their cellars and their scores. The more interesting case is the one they make for pace. A table booked for one o'clock that is still going at five. The light coming down off the mountains in the last hours of the day. A region that rewards staying in the valley over driving out from the city for an afternoon.
The clients we plan for increasingly want to come home restored rather than depleted. Not faster, not further. The Winelands are a clear expression of it.
Our latest journal piece looks at the valleys, the long lunch, and the considered way to travel them.
Come find us at No.82.
hello@agentnouveau.com
